tasks.title: Cross-Gateway Routing Automation
tasks.notes: Owner: Sanji/Suleiman. SCOPE EXPANDED: Must include automatic endpoint discovery. Every agent must know how to reach every other gateway WITHOUT hardcoded localhost assumptions. Requirements: (1) Gateway registry — single config listing all gateway IPs and ports. (2) Service discovery — agents auto-detect if single-gateway or multi-gateway setup. (3) Health-check before route — verify target is reachable before sending. (4) Fallback on failure — if primary route fails, try alternate path. (5) Must work for BOTH our 3-machine setup AND single-gateway open source setup. SPOF this patches: cross-gateway access failures like Usopp hitting 127.0.0.1 instead of 10.0.0.152. DoD: any agent on any machine can reach MC API and any other gateway without manual URL correction.

tasks.title: DealFinder — Automated Multi-Site Vehicle Search & iMessage Delivery
tasks.notes: ``` | [2026-03-26 15:59] agent: 2026-03-26 11:58 EDT — Captain directed DealFinder restructuring into a per-site data pipeline. Created file-backed site databases under /Users/minicihan/.openclaw/workspace-sanji/dealfinder/ with one CSV per source: cargurus.csv, carfax.csv, truecar.csv, autotrader.csv, edmunds.csv, carscom.csv plus README.md schema guide. New intended task pattern per site: (1) search/dump findings into the site CSV, (2) filter/rank top findings from that CSV, and then a separate delivery task sends top 5 daily without spending that session on search. Each CSV acts like a sheet: one site per file, one row per ranked finding. Next planning step: create/retask board items and cron flow so search and filtering are separated cleanly per website. | [2026-03-26 16:23] agent: 2026-03-26 12:22 EDT — Operating rule correction for fresh cron sessions: do NOT stop at a generic blocker. If browser extraction fails, the session must continue with fallback proof paths (web_search snippets, direct page fetches, route verification, CSV append with proof_type noted, ranked summary updates, and explicit baton-pass comments). A blocked tool path is not the same as a blocked task. The task is only truly blocked if no reliable extraction, verification, or documentation progress can be made after trying alternate paths. Completion standard for the chain: findings recorded in per-site CSVs, ranking/delivery evidence written, sent items marked durably, and final task trail shows nothing worthy remains unsent. | [2026-03-26 16:24] agent: 2026-03-26 12:24 EDT — Method correction: the working method for current DealFinder cron sessions is NOT browser-first. Primary method is direct machine-access search workflow: use web_search to discover current result/listing pages, use direct web_fetch/page route verification to confirm source reachability and capture available listing metadata, append proved rows into the per-site CSV immediately with proof_type noted, then rank/filter from the CSV. Browser usage is optional only if it becomes reliably available again; it is not the default path. Fresh sessions should start on the working method first so they do not waste runtime rediscovering a dead branch. | [2026-03-26 16:31] agent: 2026-03-26 12:31 EDT — Phase-order restructure approved by Captain. tasks.notes:
CAVENDISH INTERVIEW TEST 6 — FINAL SYNTHESIS REVIEW

Purpose:
Run a final review session after the first five interview tests. Judge Cavendish on consistency, judgment quality, ability to generalize principles, and whether he is ready for supervised live use.

When running this task later, ask Cavendish to do all of the following:
1. Re-read the dating system docs plus the prompts/results from interview tests 1-5.
2. Summarize the strongest repeated lessons across his own answers.
3. Identify where he was strongest and where he was still weak.
4. Rewrite one weak answer from earlier in a stronger way.
5. Explain what rules he would follow when helping Captain live so he does not become robotic, needy, or cringe.
6. Give a readiness rating: not ready / supervised only / ready for limited live help.

Expected output:
- strengths
- weaknesses
- one corrected answer
- live-help rules
- readiness rating with justification

Sanji note: do not run this yet unless Captain explicitly asks. This task exists so the six-session interview series is complete and staged. | [2026-03-27 13:05] cavendish: Session: reviewed core dating docs plus full MC board review trails for interviews 1-5, synthesized repeated principles, identified strongest zones (diagnosis, momentum management, anti-neediness, bio/platform judgment), identified weaker spots (some phrasing still slightly performative/riskier than ideal, not always enough minimal/conservative variants), rewrote one weaker low-investment answer in a cleaner form, and judged readiness as supervised only. Document findings in task notes.**

tasks.title: Paperclip Phase 1 — Escalation Wake Events + Bridge Health TTL Monitoring
tasks.notes: ### Context | [2026-03-28 16:19] nami: [routing] Report to: Telegram group -1003814818004, topic 10 (Research) | [2026-03-28 17:09] usopp: ### Context (Read Before Starting) - **Related work:** Robin research analysis at /Users/minicihan/.openclaw/workspace-usopp/PAPERCLIP-AI-SCORING-ANALYSIS.md (scored 9/10 - highest priority)   - **Captain's words:** "Process Robin Paperclip research into stories with full detail + assignments" - **Current state:** Assignment Bridge issues where ops agents can't force main agent attention. Escalation latency is hours/days instead of minutes - **Known constraints:** Need Bridge TTL monitoring to prevent false positives from natural completion delays  ### Requirements   - Build escalation wake events system that triggers when critical work is blocked too long - Implement Bridge TTL (Time To Live) monitoring with configurable thresholds   - Create one-shot cron jobs that directly ping main agents when Bridge expires - Add "flagged 4 times" pattern detection with automatic escalation wake - System must distinguish between natural delays vs stuck/ignored work  ### Definition of Done - [ ] Cron infrastructure for escalation wake events (one-shot trigger capability) - [ ] Bridge TTL monitoring with configurable timeout thresholds (default 2hr, 4hr, 8hr) - [ ] Proof: demonstration of blocked task auto-escalating to main agent attention - [ ] "Flagged 4 times" pattern detector with wake trigger - [ ] False positive prevention: natural completion vs stuck work detection - [ ] Documentation: escalation thresholds + override procedures in shared-memory/docs/ - [ ] Visual QA: Chopper tests escalation flow end-to-end, posts PASS/FAIL  ### Dependencies - **Blocked by:** EPIC-011-STORY-001 (Task Deduplication Gate) - **Blocks:** EPIC-011-STORY-003 (Safety Layer)   - **Handoff to:** Zoro security review after Sanji build complete  ### Guardrails for Task Creation **Sanji: break this into tasks following these rules:** 1. **Audit task:** Research existing cron patterns + Bridge monitoring capabilities 2. **Build task:** Implement Bridge TTL monitoring with threshold detection 3. **Trigger task:** Create one-shot escalation cron job system   4. **Pattern task:** Build "flagged 4 times" detection logic 5. **Prevention task:** Add false positive filters (natural vs stuck work) **Each task = one session. Document escalation thresholds in task notes.**

tasks.notes: ### Context | [2026-03-28 16:19] nami: [routing] Report to: Telegram group -1003814818004, topic 10 (Research) | [2026-03-28 17:25] usopp: ### Context (Read Before Starting) - **Related work:** EPIC-011-STORY-004 (Simulation Layer) provides prototypes that need comprehensive testing - **Captain's words:** "Process Robin Paperclip research into stories with full detail + assignments" - **Current state:** Paperclip prototypes exist but need rigorous QA to prove they work as intended before live rollout - **Known constraints:** Testing must validate both individual improvements AND their interactions. Must catch edge cases before they hit production  ### Requirements   - Build comprehensive QA harness for testing all Paperclip improvements (Task Dedup, Escalation Wake, Bridge Health) - Implement convergence testing to verify improvements work together without conflicts   - Create test scenarios that validate both happy path and edge cases for each improvement - Build regression testing framework to ensure new improvements don't break existing crew operations - Establish performance benchmarks and validation criteria for each Paperclip component  ### Definition of Done - [ ] QA test harness covering all Phase 1 Paperclip improvements with automated test execution - [ ] Convergence test suite validating improvements work together (no conflicts/interference) - [ ] Edge case test scenarios for each improvement (duplicate edge cases, escalation false positives, bridge timing issues) - [ ] Proof: full test execution results showing PASS/FAIL for each improvement + convergence scenarios - [ ] Regression test framework ensuring no negative impact on existing crew workflows - [ ] Performance benchmark validation (before vs after measurements for crew velocity/quality) - [ ] Test result documentation with screenshots/evidence for each test case - [ ] Bug report process: clear escalation path for any failures discovered - [ ] Documentation: QA procedures + test scenario guide in shared-memory/docs/ - [ ] Visual QA: Chopper runs full test suite, documents all results, posts comprehensive PASS/FAIL assessment  ### Dependencies - **Blocked by:** EPIC-011-STORY-004 (Simulation Layer) - need working prototypes to test - **Also blocked by:** EPIC-011-STORY-002 (Escalation Wake) - need some live systems for integration testing - **Blocks:** EPIC-011-STORY-006 (Rollout Plan) - rollout cannot proceed without QA validation - **Handoff to:** Sanji for phased rollout after Chopper QA complete  ### Guardrails for Task Creation **Chopper: break this into tasks following these rules:** 1. **Design task:** Create comprehensive test plan covering all Paperclip improvements + convergence scenarios   2. **Harness task:** Build automated QA test harness for executing test scenarios 3. **Edge case task:** Develop edge case test scenarios for each improvement (focus on failure modes) 4. **Integration task:** Test improvements working together (convergence + conflict detection) 5. **Regression task:** Validate no negative impact on existing crew operations 6. **Benchmark task:** Measure and validate performance improvements vs baseline 7. **Documentation task:** Create test results report with evidence and recommendations **Each task = one session. Screenshot everything. Document all test results in task notes.**

tasks.title: Paperclip Phased Rollout Plan + Automation Adoption
tasks.notes: ### Context | [2026-03-28 16:19] nami: [routing] Report to: Telegram group -1003814818004, topic 10 (Research) | [2026-03-28 17:25] usopp: ### Context (Read Before Starting) - **Related work:** EPIC-011-STORY-005 (QA Harness) provides validation that all Paperclip improvements are ready for production - **Captain's words:** "Process Robin Paperclip research into stories with full detail + assignments"   - **Current state:** Paperclip improvements tested and validated, need safe production rollout strategy without disrupting crew operations - **Known constraints:** Rollout must be incremental with rollback capability. Cannot deploy all improvements simultaneously - too much risk  ### Requirements   - Create phased rollout plan for deploying Paperclip improvements to live crew operations - Implement staged deployment: start with least risky improvement, monitor, then add next - Build automation adoption strategy to ensure crew agents actually use new capabilities   - Create monitoring and rollback procedures for each rollout phase - Establish success metrics and rollback triggers for each improvement  ### Definition of Done - [ ] Phased rollout plan with 3 stages: Phase A (Task Dedup), Phase B (Escalation Wake), Phase C (Bridge Health) - [ ] Deployment procedures for each phase with pre-flight checks and go/no-go criteria   - [ ] Automation adoption strategy: training materials, workflow changes, agent onboarding for new capabilities - [ ] Proof: successful deployment of Phase A with monitoring data showing improvement effectiveness - [ ] Rollback procedures tested and documented for each phase (can revert cleanly if problems) - [ ] Success metrics defined and measured: crew velocity, escalation response time, duplicate task reduction - [ ] Monitoring dashboards for tracking improvement effectiveness post-deployment - [ ] Risk mitigation: fallback procedures if any improvement causes crew disruption   - [ ] Agent training documentation: how to use new capabilities, when to escalate, what changed - [ ] Documentation: complete rollout playbook + lessons learned in shared-memory/docs/ - [ ] Visual QA: Chopper validates each rollout phase works as intended, posts PASS/FAIL for production deployment  ### Dependencies - **Blocked by:** EPIC-011-STORY-005 (QA Harness) - rollout cannot proceed without QA validation   - **Blocks:** none (final story in chain) - **Handoff to:** All department leads for adoption training after Sanji rollout complete  ### Guardrails for Task Creation **Sanji: break this into tasks following these rules:** 1. **Planning task:** Create detailed 3-phase rollout plan with success criteria and rollback triggers 2. **Phase A task:** Deploy Task Deduplication with monitoring and validation   3. **Monitor task:** Measure Phase A effectiveness, document lessons learned, go/no-go for Phase B 4. **Phase B task:** Deploy Escalation Wake Events with monitoring and validation 5. **Phase C task:** Deploy Bridge Health Monitoring with full crew integration 6. **Adoption task:** Create training materials and workflow documentation for all improvements 7. **Validation task:** Final validation that all improvements work together in production **Each task = one session. Document rollback procedures and success metrics in task notes.**
